author: @Alex_Unlocked
text: Yay! A fellow English chap, greetings from southern England 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿

author: @DavidPaulNewtonScott
text: This is going to sound nuts by hear me out I first saw las  laser cutters work as a teacher. It could only cut about 1/8 deep it occurred to me you could logs with it simply by making multiple passes. Precise and no blade to sharpen or replace. Once a log is cut to a manageable size you can cut smaller strips with a table or circular saw. This poses the question how long does the laser last.

author: @DreadMakerRoberts
text: I was recently introduced to the little Diode lasers. I did not think much of them but in the last year the advancements they have made is crazy! When you look at 20W output on a kerf line that is 1/3 the width of a Co2 version then that 20W is equivalent to a 60W Co2. Now with IR diode lasers coming out I think that Co2 lasers may be on their way out. You then no longer need to deal with mirrors and cooling systems. Anyhow just my thoughts.

replies.author: @DreadMakerRoberts
replies.text: However air assist is a MUST with these

replies.author: @generalawareness101
replies.text: I hope so as I spent 1500 USD on a CO2 laser and worked my ass off with it, and had to completely rebuild it, but I was more for engraving.  It has sat in my den (no small item either and heavy) for 3 years now.  I spent so much on it and the extra stuff (like a chiller for 600 USD, more mirrors, lenses, etc...).  I really wish I hadn't, or rather I wish it had performed as it should have for more than just cutting and it was a bit sub par with that too.  Now laser diodes still can't cut as deep as a CO2 laser for 1 pass vs 1 pass.

text: Is there going to be another competition?

text: Have you seen "Kris Harbour Natural Building" channel and the plasma cutter he uses or his water turbine making? Thought this may be of interest. Best wishes.

author: @philipvecchio3292
text: I have the Ortur laser master 2, When moving the acrylic feet broke off, and I found it difficult to get my laser stable and to get a consistent cut. Getting everything lined up is probably the biggest challenge.

author: @philipvecchio3292
text:
I know that it's possible to use this laser engraver on foods, but certification by the food and drug administration is kind of hilarious to me 😂.

My kitchen knife isn't approved by the Food and drug Administration..
